name: Iridium

overview.value: The Iridium satellite constellation provides L-band voice and data coverage to satellite phones, satellite messenger devices, and integrated transceivers. Iridium Communications owns and operates the constellation, which consists of 66 active satellites in orbit required for global coverage, plus additional spare satellites. The satellites fly in low Earth orbit at approximately 781 km altitude and 86.4° inclination, communicating with each other via Ka-band inter-satellite links to provide coverage including polar regions, oceans, and airways.

base.reason: established aggregator, single reference (audit Phase 3 backfill, 2026-07-08)

notes: Added in the audit Phase 3 backfill (2026-07-08), verify-then-add. Weekly maintenance (2026-07-13): a published Vesperio item (2026-07-05, sourced to Iridium's SEC 8-K exhibit) reports Rocket Lab agreed to acquire Iridium Communications in a cash-and-stock deal valuing it at about $8 billion enterprise value, both boards approved, close expected mid-2027 subject to Iridium stockholder and regulatory approval; Iridium separately completed its buyout of the remaining 61% of Aireon on 2026-07-06. operator/status fields left unchanged since the deal has not closed. Re-checked satellite counts via a fresh Wikipedia fetch: the constellation-status table there gives an ambiguous/inconsistent active-satellite breakdown that conflicts with the existing 66-active figure already in this profile's own overview; not trusted enough to overwrite, existing sats_launched_total (80, NEXT-generation only) was independently corroborated by the same fetch ('80 (NEXT)').

events.headline: Iridium launches its first five constellation satellites on a Delta II
Iridium launches first ten Iridium NEXT satellites on Falcon 9
Iridium launches another batch of Iridium NEXT satellites on Falcon 9
SpaceX completes Iridium NEXT constellation deployment (per SpaceNews)
Rocket Lab agrees to acquire Iridium in $8 billion cash-and-stock deal
Iridium completes acquisition of remaining 61% of Aireon for about $367 million (per SpaceNews)
